module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:42–44: one-sentence lead-in + {{prompt:ae101-m2-integrate-branch}} marker. Body describes the move as a lead-in, not as a substitute for the prompt. Registry entry confirmed at curriculum/prompts/ae101-m2-integrate-branch.md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages room outside text
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:28 'The room harvests everyone's before the lecture names the pattern.' — Connections room-harvest carve-out documented in maintainer block line 84 and compounded/2026-05-03-pedagogy-connections-room-read-carveout.md. No other cohort-management sentences in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need forced-engagement mechanic
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plan-mode approval inflation addressed via two mandatory push-backs (forcing function in push-back-on-the-plan.md exercise). Strategy M2 detail: 'force-engagement: rewrite or reject at least one before saving' on task-shaping rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task-selection names floor AND ceiling explicitly
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:10 '**Just a simple task that spans a few files. Not an epic.**' — bolded one-line anti-pattern callout present in Prework.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 114–117: three falsifiable outcomes (Shift+Tab before approving; push-back quotes step number + specific word; Q-and-A sequence in scrollback). Each has 'Falsifiable:' qualifier.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced artefact has a contract row
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 119–124: contract table present for task-shaping rule file (stable identifier: student-picked path; produced by: Exercise 2; consumed by: M4 walk-and-fill Phase 1) and personal rules update (CLAUDE.local.md).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 126–138: nine rows covering P1, P3 (x3), P4 (x2), P5 (x2), and Compound. Each row names dominant failure + one recovery move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 close has '## Pre-reads before Module 3' (lines 59–65) with Willison + OWASP links. earn-the-trust.md (M3) line 10 has matching Prework reference to the same two readings.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:30 'When a plan is good' before exercises = arming/opener lecture (gives diagnostic lenses + operational spirit). line:36 'Where the rule could live' after exercises = meta-frame closer naming three rule-shapes. Confirmed by strategy M2-in-detail: 'Mini-lecture — where the rule could live (~4–5 min, meta-frame closer).'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 mood is grounded competence, not a contrast-mood module. Contrast-mood rule applies to M5-style modules. N/A here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 79: 'Mood target: grounded competence'. Strategy M2-in-detail confirms same. Trace phase_score=8 and close_score=8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood, not punitive
rules_evaluated.evidence: Connections question (line 26–27) frames approval inflation as universal ('We all do it') — not a blame frame. No 'you did it wrong' register anywhere in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3–4) leads with positive discipline ('Reading a plan is finite... Paired, they give a complete read'). Key Concepts bullet 4: 'Plan mode is a permission state, not a mood.' Discipline-first throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pocock grill-me move inlined as plain prompt per strategy M2-in-detail: 'inlined as a plain prompt so M2 doesn't front-run M3's first-Skill-use moment.' line:57 Next/Bridge names skill authoring for M3 but does not reveal conversation-based authoring or weakest-part self-critique (M3's felt experience).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:50 '**Assume 90% on first pass.**' (theme: 90% correct). line:49 '**Push-backs ship into memory.**' (theme: compounding). Two theme breadcrumbs on distinct bullets. Minimum 2-3 met.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — gates run before breadcrumbs
rules_evaluated.evidence: All Key Concepts bullets lead with discipline (rule 3), none front-run downstream modules (rule 4). Breadcrumbs on bullet 3 and bullet 4 decorate survivors only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:42 'Ask Claude to integrate the one branch that earned itself into your personal ./CLAUDE.local.md, or to say so and stop if nothing did.' — action lead-in present and under 20 words with command verb.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module files reference by marker
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:44 {{prompt:ae101-m2-integrate-branch}} marker. curriculum/prompts/ae101-m2-integrate-branch.md confirmed present with correct frontmatter (key, dest, context, runtime, origin).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands 8+ at every teaching beat
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single-phase module (no ## Phase headers). Phase mood_score=8; close mood_score=8. 'The room harvests' line (line 28) is mood-exempt (sequencing/transition beat). All other beats are teaching beats scoring 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3 teaching moment unviolated
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:57 Next/Bridge: 'the same move goes into durable infrastructure: the judges and gates you ship to your team kit catch exactly the kind of branch the second read surfaced today.' M3's felt experience (conversation-based skill authoring + weakest-part self-critique) is entirely unpreempted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — plan-mode approval inflation fires reliably across persona-skill variation
rules_evaluated.evidence: Connections question (line 26–27) is persona-specific and direct: 'when was the last time Claude wrote a plan you approved without really reading?' For engineer persona using CC daily for 2 weeks, this fires immediately — no alternative interpretation, no way to skip. The forcing function is the push-back requirement in the referenced exercise (structural, not body prose).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity to strategy M2
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3–4) maps directly to strategy M2-in-detail core exercise: two reads (human push-back + agent walk-down) catch different things; paired gives the complete read. 'Neither alone does' matches strategy's 'plan-mode approval inflation catches as the key teaching moment.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M2 close hands off to M3 open
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 57–58: Bridge hands off to M3 ('durable infrastructure: judges and gates') without giving away M3's authoring felt-experience. Pre-reads before M3 appear symmetrically in M2 close + M3 Prework (rule 28 confirmed). M3 strategy open hook (Connections) is unviolated.
